title: News & Events - 211info
text: Explore the latest community news and events and get to know the people behind 211info. For all media inquiries or interview requests, please contact: Dan Herman [email protected] 360-521-6527 Food insecurity is a growing concern for many as prices continue to climb. But for older adults on fixed incomes, even basics like fresh, healthy food can feel out of reach. That’s where the Senior Farm Direct Nutrition Program (SFDNP) can help. SFDNP provides eligible seniors with vouchers to buy locally grown fruits, vegetables, and... Read More Whether you are financially planning ahead, catching up after the holidays, or trying to make sense of tax season, free and low-cost resources are available to help with both financial literacy and tax preparation. This includes help understanding tax credits, finding reliable filing options, and building knowledge that supports long-term stability and financial resilience. Knowing... Read More For our Community Outreach and Engagement Team, the core of the work is meeting communities where they are, listening first, and learning how to make it easier for people to understand how 211info exists to remove the guesswork. Through relationship-building, local presence, and targeted engagement, our team works to ensure that 211info is understood as... Read More As of January 1, 2026, 211info serves all 36 counties in Oregon, and Washington 211 (WA 211) serves communities in Southwest Washington. This change reflects WA 211’s transition from multiple regional 211 offices to a centralized, statewide hub. Oregon already operates under a similar model, with a single point of coordination statewide. 211info previously managed... Read More Every family’s situation is different, and finding the right help can be complicated and overwhelming. That’s where connecting with 211 comes in. In addition to 211info’s everyday call, text, and email services, we also offer specialized resource navigation to support child and family health and well-being. These focused areas offer one-on-one guidance from staff who... Read More Each day, 211info staff show up with kindness, determination, and an extraordinary talent for listening to understand, that is driven by their desire to help others. Read on to learn more about three of our staff members, and discover the heart of 211info. Read More Learn about additional resources available during the winter months, plus volunteering and donation opportunities for those looking to help others this season. The winter season means additional resources become available for the community, and it’s a period of high call volume at 211info, when many people look for additional help, and also ways to give... Read More PORTLAND, Ore. – 211info has achieved Inform USA Accreditation, recognizing the organization’s commitment to delivering the highest standard of Information & Referral (I&R) services to communities across Oregon and Southwest Washington. The accreditation follows a comprehensive, third-party evaluation by Inform USA, the national leader in I&R standards. The process reviews all aspects of agency operations,... Read More Calling 211 is the fastest way to find local clean air spaces and cooling centers. We provide swift connections to essential resources, support services, and up-to-date local information. Our specially trained teams ensure our database is timely and accurate, and that a live person helps each caller. Phone interpretation is available in over 240 languages.... Read More Updated: July 2, 2025 4:40pm PORTLAND, Ore., July 1, 2025 — 211info, the private nonprofit that manages the 211 system for Oregon and Southwest Washington, announced today that it will reduce its core phone service hours after July 4, 2025. Regular phone hours will shift from 24/7 operations, to Monday through Friday, 8 AM to... Read More 1 2 3 4 Next > Search Popular Posts Categories Equity News
